title: Local Repo Development
description: Use when making local code, docs, config, or test changes that need repository topology discovery, agent instruction files, project specs/docs, git worktree or branch setup, validation, review, secret scanning, commits, PR/MR submission, or CI/CD follow-up.

input_schema.feature_name: string — short feature, fix, or task name used to identify the worktree and branch
input_schema.repo_path: string — local repository path, usually the current working directory
input_schema.commit_intent: string — optional summary of the changes that should be committed
input_schema.mr_url: string — optional merge request or pull request URL created for the work

repository_topologies.Single repo: One git root, no nested .git, and no workspace manifest covering sibling packages; use root instructions plus nearest package/module instructions.
repository_topologies.Monorepo/workspace: Workspace manifest such as pnpm-workspace.yaml, package.json workspaces, turbo.json, nx.json, lerna.json, rush.json, Cargo workspace, go.work, pyproject workspace, or Bazel files; identify affected packages and run package-scoped plus root gates.
repository_topologies.Multi-repo task: Request, docs, scripts, or config references sibling repos/services; treat each repo as separately owned with independent instructions, dirty state, branch/worktree decision, and validation.
repository_topologies.Nested repo/submodule: git submodule status, nested .git, or superproject working tree is present; report parent/submodule status separately and do not edit nested repo as parent content.
repository_topologies.Generated/vendor subtree: vendor/generated/cache paths or docs mark generated code; do not manually edit unless repo instructions allow it.

spec_doc_artifacts.Product/spec: spec.md, SPECS.md, docs/spec*, requirements.md, prd.md, rfcs/*, adr/*
spec_doc_artifacts.Development workflow: CONTRIBUTING.md, DEVELOPMENT.md, docs/development*, Makefile, task runner config

knowledge_update_targets: AGENTS.md or equivalent agent instruction files for durable agent workflow constraints
CONTRIBUTING.md or DEVELOPMENT.md for build/test/dev workflow changes
README.md for user-facing setup or high-level project behavior
docs/architecture*, adr/*, or docs/design* for durable architectural decisions
docs/spec*, requirements.md, or RFC/ADR files for accepted product/spec changes
instructions: Confirm the current directory is inside a git repository with git rev-parse --show-toplevel; if not, ask for the repository path.
Classify repository topology as single repo, monorepo/workspace, multi-repo task, nested repo/submodule, or generated/vendor subtree before editing.
For multi-repo or nested-repo work, build a per-repo plan before editing any repo.

Search from each repo root to target paths for instruction files such as AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, GEMINI.MD, .cursorrules, .cursor/rules/*, .github/copilot-instructions.md, README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and DEVELOPMENT.md.

Read applicable instruction files before choosing implementation or validation commands; nearest path-specific instructions override broader ones unless they conflict with the user's explicit request.

Discover relevant specs/docs/contracts/workflow files such as spec.md, docs/spec*, requirements, PRDs, RFCs, ADRs, architecture docs, development docs, API schemas, migrations, runbooks, release docs, and CI workflows.
If no relevant spec/docs exist for a risky change, state that and use repository code/tests as the source of truth.
Before editing, inspect git worktree list --porcelain and git rev-parse --git-dir to determine whether the current checkout is already a suitable linked worktree for the task.
If not in a suitable linked worktree, derive a kebab-case slug from the feature name and create a sibling worktree from origin/main, then origin/master, then local main/master, then HEAD.
Create a branch such as feature/<slug> or fix/<slug> and use a non-destructive unique path if the preferred branch or path already exists.
Never discard existing user changes; work around unrelated dirty files and ask only when they block the task.

Implement the requested change using discovered repository instructions, specs/docs, build conventions, style rules, and test conventions; in multi-repo work, keep each repo's constraints and validation separate.

When the change creates or invalidates durable repo knowledge, update the appropriate agent instruction, development, README, architecture, ADR, spec, or requirements file; do not add transient notes, private reasoning, secrets, local paths, or duplicated facts.
Before committing, perform a code review pass over git diff HEAD or the staged diff and fix material findings.
Before committing, scan local repository changes for sensitive information with gitleaks, detect-secrets, or a focused fallback scan; never print secret values.
Stage only intentional files and commit only after code review and sensitive information scan pass; in multi-repo work, commit and report per repository unless the user explicitly asks otherwise.
After submitting a merge request or pull request, asynchronously track CI/CD until it passes, fails, or is canceled; inspect failing job logs and fix in-scope failures.

Report topology classification, affected repositories, applicable instruction files, relevant spec/docs files, selected repo root, branch, worktree status, changed files, validation commands, diff review result, sensitive information scan result, knowledge-file updates, commit hash when created, PR/MR URL when created, and CI/CD state when observed.
Do not claim a clean worktree, successful test, secret scan, commit, push, PR/MR, or passing CI/CD without observed evidence.

examples.input: Add a dashboard filter feature in /repo/app.
examples.output: Classified /repo/app as a monorepo package change, read root AGENTS.md, package README.md, and docs/filters-spec.md, confirmed current checkout was not a linked worktree, created ../app-dashboard-filter from origin/main on branch feature/dashboard-filter, implemented the change under the package's test conventions, updated docs/filters-spec.md because the accepted filter behavior changed, reviewed the diff, ran validation and a secret scan, committed only the intended files, opened an MR, and asynchronously monitored CI/CD until the pipeline passed.
